channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI bots could be a new tool to get people to be open about their feelings
item.description: As the legislative election in France approached this summer, a research team decided to reach out to hundreds of citizens to interview them about their views on key issues. But the interviewer asking the questions wasn’t a human researcher — it was an AI chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Kathleen Davis, Fast Company

item.title: Roblox gives parents more control over kids' experience
item.description: Roblox, which has been downloaded millions of times, announced sweeping and restrictive settings for minor safety.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: How to easily record phone calls on your Android
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals an easy trick to record a phone conversation on an Android. Once completed, recordings are easy to find.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia: Growth Prospects Remain Strong Amid Cooling Challenges
item.description: Nvidia's growth prospects remain strong despite cooling challenges, making it an attractive investment option in the tech sector.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: 5 ways AI should be fixing travel planning right now
item.description: Recently, I attempted to plan a road trip through Canada. What should have been exciting—imagining stunning national parks and scenic drives—became a digital nightmare of juggling 50 different websites.
item.dc:creator: Peter Smart, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk attacks Australian government over proposed social media ban
item.description: An Australian Cabinet minister on Friday rejected X Corp. owner Elon Musk’s allegation that the government intended to control all Australians’ access to the internet through legislation that would ban young children from social media.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX will deliver its first lunar terrain vehicle to the Moon by 2029
item.description: Hot on the heels of another productive test flight, two new lunar customers have been added to the flight manifest for SpaceX’s Starship. Eagle landing Lunar Outpost said on Thursday that SpaceX will deliver its first Lunar Terrain Vehicle, Eagle, to the Moon by 2029.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz, Fast Company

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Gallagher not interested in serving as SEC chair in second Trump administration
item.description: Dan Gallagher, chief legal officer at the financial services firm Robinhood, said Friday that he is not interested in chairing the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) in President-elect Trump’s second administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The future according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis
item.description: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis used to get some of his most fulfilling work done between midnight and 3 a.m. A self-described “nocturnal person,” the Londoner devoted the solitude of his overnight shift to catching up on scientific papers, coming up with new ideas, and just plain thinking.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: DARPA tries a simple but profound concept to improve cybersecurity
item.description: "This idea of compartmentalization has a realization by breaking systems up into small pieces," said Howard Strobe. The post DARPA tries a simple but profound concept to improve cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon to invest an additional $4 billion in AI startup Anthropic
item.description: Amazon is investing an additional $4 billion in the artificial intelligence startup Anthropic as major technology companies rush to fund generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: From vision to value: A strategic approach to generative AI adoption
item.description: Generative AI has the potential to redefine productivity, create novel applications, and reinvent customer experience.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elias: JD Vance’s Bay Area connections all but guaranteed him his veep post
item.description: It's a much more complex story than what happened among Democrats, even if Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz was even more obscure than Vance before last summer.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Elias, The Mercury News

item.title: Pentagon Deploys New Private 5G Strategy to Fill Global Connectivity Gaps
item.description: Pentagon deploys new private 5G strategy to fill global connectivity gaps, leveraging commercial and public infrastructure for military operations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Hypersonic jet promises NYC to London in under an hour
item.description: A Texas company is developing a hypersonic jet, Stargazer, that could fly from New York City to London in under an hour, redefining boundaries of aviation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CIOs contend with gen AI growing pains
item.description: The road ahead for IT leaders in turning the promise of generative AI into business value remains steep and daunting, but the key components of the gen AI roadmap — data, platform, and skills — are evolving and becoming better defined.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AWS prepares to command an army of AI agents
item.description: According to a recent report from SNS Insider, the global AI agents market was valued at $3.7 billion in 2023. The market is expected to grow to $103.6 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 44.9% during the forecast period from 2024 to 2032.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australia rejects Elon Musk's claim that it plans to control access to the internet
item.description: An Australian Cabinet minister has rejected X Corp. owner Elon Musk’s allegation that the government intends to control all Australians' access to the internet through legislation that would ban young children from social media.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tecnología de Inteligencia Artificial: Gestionando Costos para Éxito
item.description: Aprende cómo los CIO pueden subestimar costos de IA y estrategias para su implementación exitosa.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Thai Court Rejects Spyware Lawsuit Against Israeli Tech Firm
item.description: Thai court dismisses lawsuit against NSO Group over alleged use of Pegasus spyware to hack activist's phone
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gary Gensler said he’ll step down as SEC chair. Bitcoin is soaring
item.description: Crypto enthusiasts are ecstatic after U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair Gary Gensler said he will step down from his post on January 20 once President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Harnessing AI: A NetApp perspective
item.description: We take pride in our heritage at NetApp. Our heritage is rooted in developing innovative solutions that address the challenges of storing, managing, and protecting data in a complex IT environment.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nile unwraps NaaS security features for enterprise customers
item.description: Network-as-a-service startup Nile has added new zero-trust capabilities and tapped Microsoft and Palo Alto Networks to bolster its enterprise security services. Nile was founded by former Cisco CEO John Chambers and Pankaj Patel, Cisco’s former chief development officer.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Backup Software vs. Backup as a Service (BaaS): The Key Differences
item.description: As data volumes increase exponentially, the threat of data loss from cyberattacks, human error, or system failure has never been greater — which is why, in 2025, fortifying a data protection strategy has never made more sense.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: SEC chair to step down on Inauguration Day
item.description: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair Gary Gensler will step down from his role when President-elect Trump takes office on Jan. 20, he announced Thursday. “The Securities and Exchange Commission is a remarkable agency,” Gensler said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta's Threads app launches custom feeds amid Bluesky surge
item.description: The rollout came just days after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced last week that Threads was testing the new feature.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: How the U.S. Space Force can modernize at a smarter pace
item.description: Modernizing space programs while maintaining operations is one of the most challenging things, and rings true for the U.S. Space Force. The post How the U.S. Space Force can modernize at a smarter pace first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Cisco: Pressure to deploy AI is up, but only 13% feel ready
item.description: Pressure to implement AI plans is on the rise, but the readiness of enterprise networks to handle AI workloads has actually declined over the past year, according to new research from Cisco.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: You’ll soon be able to clone your voice to speak other languages ​​in Teams
item.description: In connection with this year’s Ignite conference, Microsoft has unveiled a new interpretation tool that will be added to Teams in the spring.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cybersecurity Tax Credit Proposed for Smaller Defense Contractors
item.description: Draft legislation aims to help smaller businesses cover costs of complying with the Department of Defense's Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification program.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft Ignite 2024 — get the latest news and insights
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 kicks off in Chicago and runs Nov. 19-22. If you can’t make it to Chicago, no worries. First, the physical event is sold out, according to the Ignite event page. Second, it’s a hybrid event, so you can attend Ignite virtually.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: GSA listening more to customers to improve IAE platforms
item.description: Memi Whitehead, the deputy assistant commissioner for GSA’s Integrated Award Environment, said new tools aim to improve its users’ experiences. The post GSA listening more to customers to improve IAE platforms first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: US commission proposes ‘Manhattan Project-like’ initiative for AI
item.description: A US congressional commission has called for a “Manhattan Project-like” initiative to accelerate artificial intelligence (AI) development, urging Congress to grant the executive branch sweeping, multiyear contracting authority to fund advancements in AI, cloud computing, and data centers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: IBM deal brings AMD Instinct accelerators to IBM Cloud
item.description: IBM is expanding its AI accelerator options for enterprise users of its cloud service. AMD Instinct MI300X accelerators are now available as a service on IBM Cloud, the vendors announced.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PIX 11
